heading: Sixto timing-graph curves now have an exact analytic packet on the QA substrate
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto, timing-graph, law-packet, exact-interpolation]
body: Built `sixto_graph_curve_law_packet.json`, `sixto_graph_curve_law_diagnostics.json`, and `build_sixto_graph_curve_law_packet.py` to promote the recovered Sixto timing-graph curves from sampled scene polylines into an explicit analytic law packet on `qa_rational_pair_noreduce`. The accepted packet is exact Newton interpolation on the recovered sample nodes for all four traces (`curve_graph_light_green`, `curve_graph_green`, `curve_graph_cyan`, `curve_graph_blue`), with exact node validation true for all 4 curves and degree 16 in each case. Simpler global candidates were tested and rejected as non-exact: affine, quadratic, cubic, quartic, and a two-mode harmonic family. Best near-misses still had nonzero node error, for example the harmonic family reached max abs error about `15.074526` on the green curve and `16.348952` on the blue curve, but did not land exactly. Honest verdict: the timing graph now has a closed analytic packet exact on the recovered sample nodes, but this is still an interpolation law rather than a validated minimal source-native dynamics law.
